element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Button sequence initiation leads to automated multi-hit combos
Counter mechanic to escape opponent combos at auto-double or linker stage
Each player has 2 bars; match ends when both depleted, no health recovery between bars
Character-specific execution to finish defeated opponent
High-damage combo finisher requiring sustained input execution
Unique command inputs per character (Jago's Tiger Spirit techniques, etc.)

typical_relief_triggers: Entering second health bar with advantage
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Round-by-round escalation with health bar breakpoints; tension sustained through combo chains with defensive counterplay windows

difficulty_curve.early_game: Training/Tutorial mode introduces basic combat fundamentals (attack buttons, basic combos)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Tournament progression introduces combo breaker mechanics and character-specific special moves; CPU opponent AI increases read capability
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounters (Eyedol) emphasize pattern recognition and advanced combo execution; opponent uses full move arsenal
notable_spikes: First double health bar match (requires adapting to no-recovery mechanic)
Introduction of combo breaker timing windows
Final boss Eyedol (dimensional creature with distinct moveset)

notable_level_patterns.description: Sequential single-elimination matches against 10 playable characters before Eyedol boss; each character has distinct fighting style and special moves
Match structure requires depleting two full health bars per opponent with no recovery between bars; creates strategic breakpoints at first bar depletion
Opponent attack rhythm cycles between auto-doubles and linker moves, creating specific windows for breaker counter-execution

sources_used: tcrf - Killer Instinct (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
